ail: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aimele.
ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
anil: noun. The indigo shrub; or the indigo dye obtained from the plant.
anvil: noun. A heavy iron block used in the blacksmithing trade as a surface upon which metal can be struck and shaped.
cavil: verb. To criticise for petty or frivolous reasons.
clan: noun. A group of people all descended from a common ancestor, in fact or belief, especially when the exact genealogies are not known.
clivia: noun. Any plant of the genus Clivia, native to southern Africa.
incivil: adjective. Displaying a lack of courtesy; rude, impolite.
lac: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lacandon.
laic: noun. A layperson as opposed to a cleric, i.e. not a member of the clergy.
lav: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Latvian.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
nail: noun. The thin, horny plate at the ends of fingers and toes on humans, chimpanzees and some other animals.
nil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nila.
nival: adjective. Abounding with snow; snowy; snow-covered.
vac: noun. Clipping of vacation (“extended period of time away from work or school”).
vain: adjective. Overly proud of oneself, especially concerning appearance; having a high opinion of one's own accomplishments with slight reason.
van: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Valman.
via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vial: noun. A glass or plastic vessel or bottle, especially a small tube-shaped bottle used to store medicine, perfume or other chemicals.
vicinal: adjective. Of or pertaining to a neighborhood; neighboring.
vinca: noun. Any of several evergreen shrubs, of the genus Vinca, including the periwinkle.
vinic: adjective. Of or pertaining to wine.
